title: First Heirloom Harvest! 🍅
content: Finally picked my first Cherokee Purple tomato of the season. Incredible flavor! Tips on preventing skin cracking in this heat?

title: Aphid Invasion! 🐜
content: Found these little guys on my roses. Anyone have a DIY spray recipe that actually works?

title: Monstera Monday 🌿
content: New leaf unfurling today! The fenestrations are getting better with every new shoot.

title: Compost Tea Recipe
content: Brewing a fresh batch of black gold for the spring veggies. Your soil will thank you later.

title: Propagating Lavender
content: Taking softwood cuttings today. Fingers crossed for high success rates this year!

title: Peony Blooms are here!
content: These only last a week but they are the highlight of my garden every single year. 🌸

title: Balcony Garden Hack
content: Used vertical hanging shoe racks for my herbs. Saved so much floor space!

title: Frost Warning ❄️
content: Covering my tender greens tonight. Don't let the surprise frost take your hard work!

title: Pruning Shears Review
content: Finally invested in some high-end bypass pruners. My wrists are much happier now.

title: Wildflower Path Design
content: Starting to sketch out the new cottage garden layout. Thinking of a curved gravel path.

title: Slug Defense 🐌
content: Copper tape or beer traps? What's your go-to move for protecting Hostas?

title: Pepper Sprouting!
content: My Habaneros just poked their heads above the soil. Spring is officially here.

title: Spider Plant Babies
content: This plant is literally throwing children at me. Who wants some cuttings? Free to a good home!

title: Testing Soil pH
content: Found out my soil is way too alkaline for my blueberries. Time to add some sulfur.

title: Pothos in Water
content: Day 14 of water prop. We have significant root development. Shoots coming soon!

title: Lavender Field Dreams
content: Planning to line the entire driveway with Hidcote lavender. The scent will be amazing.

title: Chickens in the Garden
content: The girls are doing a great job tilling the spent vegetable beds today. Good fertilizer too!

title: Mulching 101
content: Applying a thick layer of straw to keep the moisture in before the summer heat hits.

title: Best Watering Can?
content: Looking for something with a long neck and a fine rose. Any recommendations?

title: Native Plant Corner
content: Finished the xeriscape section of the backyard. No more watering the lawn for me!
